message:
test: pin runtime policy env lock recovery

Closes bd-4rjgy

files.patch:

Printf_conformance still blocked by memory bugs (double-free/SIGSEGV) in core printf implementation - needs separate investigation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T16:27:39.120736973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T16:35:48.879912269Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T16:35:48.877607670Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","printf","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-4qqu","title":"resolver: apply AI_ADDRCONFIG to null-node results","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:14:38.988614093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:18:03.268036952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:18:03.265719675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4rdz8","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] aio_suspend panics on adversarial timespec (negative tv_sec / out-of-range tv_nsec) instead of returning EINVAL","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:7934-7940 (aio_suspend), and by delegation aio_suspend64:15597.

Current code:
    let ts = unsafe { *timeout };
    let dur = std::time::Duration::new(ts.tv_sec as u64, ts.tv_nsec as u32);
    Some(std::time::Instant::now() + dur)

Defect:
1. `ts.tv_sec as u64` reinterprets a negative i64 (e.g. tv_sec = -1) as u64::MAX, producing a near-infinite Duration.
2. `ts.tv_nsec as u32` truncates: a negative tv_nsec or a value >= 2**32 is silently accepted.
3. `Instant::now() + dur` for a near-u64::MAX Duration panics with 'overflow when adding duration to instant', aborting the process.

POSIX contract: aio_suspend shall fail with EINVAL if timeout->tv_nsec < 0 or >= 1_000_000_000. A negative tv_sec is also invalid.

Severity: HIGH. Reachable from any C program calling aio_suspend with attacker-controlled (or merely corrupted) timespec — this is in the exported libc.so surface. An adversarial aio_suspend(NULL-list-with-nent=0 already rejected, else valid-list, &{.tv_sec=-1}) aborts the whole process.

Fix plan:
- Validate tv_sec >= 0 and tv_nsec in [0, 999_999_999]; return -1 with EINVAL otherwise.
- Use Instant::checked_add and fall back to 'no deadline' on overflow (which then polls every 100ms until completion, matching the no-timeout path).
- Add a regression unit test in the abi crate that passes an out-of-range timespec and asserts -1/EINVAL instead of panic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:41:47.617220787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:44:10.501229724Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T23:44:10.499013410Z","close_reason":"Fixed: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs aio_suspend now validates tv_sec >= 0 and tv_nsec in [0, 999_999_999], returning -1/EINVAL on violation, and uses Instant::checked_add to avoid the Duration-add panic on very large (but technically valid) tv_sec. Four regression tests added in crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/unistd_abi_test.rs (aio_suspend_rejects_negative_tv_sec_without_panic / _negative_tv_nsec / _oversize_tv_nsec / _empty_list_before_timeout). Run: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_review cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test unistd_abi_test aio_suspend -> 4/0 passed in 0.00s. cargo check --all-targets clean. aio_suspend64 inherits the fix via its unsafe { aio_suspend(...) } delegation at line 15597.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-4rjgy","title":"[MODE-5][HIGH] symbolic correctness: runtime_policy env lock poisoning hides root failures","description":"[MODE-5][HIGH] symbolic correctness: runtime_policy tests collapse into lock poisoning after the first assertion failure

Fresh remote validation on the current tree:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- current failing crate path: `frankenlibc-abi::runtime_policy::tests`

Observed failures:
- `mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt` fails its primary assertion at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:1865-1867` because the exported log does not contain `runtime_mode_switch_attempt`.
- after that failure, three more tests panic at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:1699-1703` because `env_lock()` uses `.expect("env lock should not be poisoned")` on a shared `Mutex<()>`.

This turns one behavioral failure into four red tests and destroys fault isolation inside the suite.

Fix direction:
- fix the missing mode-switch logging behavior in `mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt`
- make `env_lock()` recover from poisoning in tests, for example with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`, so one failure does not mask the rest of the signal.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:48:11.314520118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:45:34.576417806Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","review","runtime-policy","symbolic"]}
+{"id":"bd-4rjgy","title":"[MODE-5][HIGH] symbolic correctness: runtime_policy env lock poisoning hides root failures","description":"[MODE-5][HIGH] symbolic correctness: runtime_policy tests collapse into lock poisoning after the first assertion failure

Fresh remote validation on the current tree:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- current failing crate path: `frankenlibc-abi::runtime_policy::tests`

Observed failures:
- `mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt` fails its primary assertion at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:1865-1867` because the exported log does not contain `runtime_mode_switch_attempt`.
- after that failure, three more tests panic at `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/runtime_policy.rs:1699-1703` because `env_lock()` uses `.expect("env lock should not be poisoned")` on a shared `Mutex<()>`.

This turns one behavioral failure into four red tests and destroys fault isolation inside the suite.

Fix direction:
- fix the missing mode-switch logging behavior in `mode_logging_captures_startup_selection_and_switch_attempt`
- make `env_lock()` recover from poisoning in tests, for example with `unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`, so one failure does not mask the rest of the signal.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:48:11.314520118Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:22:53.044490370Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:22:53.042396632Z","close_reason":"Added runtime_policy env-lock unwind regression coverage; verified mode switch logging and ABI tests via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["modes","review","runtime-policy","symbolic"]}
 {"id":"bd-4rl","title":"Closure gate: block bead completion without test commands, logs, and evidence links","description":"Goal: enforce quality by policy, not convention.

Deliverables:
- Automated gate that validates required bead evidence before status can move to closed.
- Evidence checklist includes unit, e2e, logs, and (if perf-related) baseline/profile/isomorphism data.
- Human-readable failure diagnostics for missing evidence.

Acceptance:
- Attempting to close a bead without required evidence fails deterministically.
- Gate is applied to all critique-response workstreams.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): All deliverables verified: (1) check_closure_gate.sh validates matrix entries, test commands, artifact refs, coverage status, and close_blockers for all closed critique beads; (2) Evidence checklist includes unit_cmds, e2e_cmds, log_schema_refs, perf_proof_refs, artifact_paths, conformance_fixtures; (3) Human-readable diagnostics with bead-specific issue codes (no_matrix_entry, no_test_commands, etc.); (4) CI integration in scripts/ci.sh. Gate pass + 7 closure_gate_test pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:39:53.833715526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:49:58.864777Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:49:58.864777Z","close_reason":"Closure gate fully operational. scripts/check_closure_gate.sh validates 5 required evidence criteria with CI blocking. 7 harness tests pass. 8/8 non-exempt closed beads have complete evidence. +{"id":"bd-4z5o3","title":"FUZZ: splice/sendfile/tee/vmsplice/copy_file_range harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_splice.rs. Zero-copy IO family — classic offset/size arithmetic pitfalls. Walks splice(in_pipe, out_pipe, len, flags), sendfile(out, in_file, *offset, count), tee(in_pipe, out_pipe, len, flags), vmsplice(pipe, iovec[], nr_segs, flags), copy_file_range(in_fd, *off_in, out_fd, *off_out, len, flags). Scratch fds: two pipes + a memfd. Invariants: rc <= requested len; offsets advance iff kernel documented to; no panic on giant/negative len.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:22:26.196729193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:22:26.196729193Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Current coverage: 6 cases. Target: +10 cases covering sigset_t operations.","status":"done","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:49:31.518049031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:51:37.299272125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-z4k96","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] locale_abi.rs setlocale/newlocale/textdomain/bindtextdomain walk unbounded memory via CStr::from_ptr on user-controlled pointers","description":"Location: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/locale_abi.rs

Four POSIX/GNU locale entrypoints read user-supplied string pointers through CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A caller that passes a non-NUL-terminated pointer walks arbitrary process memory until an accidental zero byte — info-leak or segfault, reachable from the libc.so boundary.

Affected sites:
- setlocale line 125: `let name = unsafe { CStr::from_ptr(locale) }.to_bytes();`
- textdomain lines 351, 355: dereferences `*domainname` (line 351) then CStr::from_ptr (line 355) — both on user-supplied pointers.
- bindtextdomain lines 377, 391: CStr::from_ptr on both `domainname` and `dirname`.
- newlocale line 441: CStr::from_ptr on `locale`.

Other parts of this file (lines 662, 741, 750, 759) read known-terminated internal strings and are fine.

Reachability: setlocale is called by essentially every C/C++ program at startup; newlocale is used in POSIX.1-2008 thread-safe locale code; textdomain/bindtextdomain are used by every gettext-enabled program. An adversarial caller (or corrupted pointer) triggers an unbounded memory walk.

Severity: HIGH. The same pattern was just fixed in __assert_perror_fail (bd-9bijw); locale entry points are more commonly invoked, so this is at least as reachable.

Fix plan:
- Introduce a shared read_bounded_cstr helper that wraps scan_c_string + known_remaining and returns Option<Vec<u8>>.
- Replace each CStr::from_ptr in the four functions with read_bounded_cstr; on None, treat the locale/domain as invalid (null return / query-mode default) per each function's existing contract.
- Also null-check *domainname byte read in textdomain by reading through the helper instead of dereferencing directly.

Tests: setlocale/newlocale are not in the abort-barrier category — add a regression test that feeds a non-NUL-terminated buffer via a short page-aligned slice and asserts the return is null without walking past the allocation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:21:23.751231844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:21:27.488804539Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-z7gt","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Iconv conformance+reproducibility gates with structured evidence","description":"Background:
- iconv completion claims require end-to-end evidence, not just generator/runtime unit tests.

Goal:
- Deliver conformance + reproducibility verification pack for iconv pipeline and runtime behavior.

Deliverables:
1) Conversion fixtures for included codecs.
2) Reproducibility gate artifacts for generated tables.
3) Drift checks tying docs/support matrix to implementation reality.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Conversion fixtures and reproducibility gates pass in CI.
- Evidence artifacts are complete and reviewable.

Verification & Logging:
- E2E fixture scripts with strict/hardened runs where relevant.
- Structured logs: trace_id, fixture_id, codec_pair, expected, actual, timing, artifact_refs.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Mandatory Verification Gate
1. Unit Test Evidence
- Add deterministic unit tests for nominal, boundary, and invalid-input paths in the touched modules.
- Include strict and hardened mode assertions when behavior differs by runtime mode.
- Include failure-mode tests covering repair/deny/default behavior with explicit expected outputs.

2. Integration + E2E Evidence
- Add or extend fixture-driven integration coverage in `tests/conformance` and/or `tests/integration` for this bead scope.
- Add a deterministic E2E script path (or harness command) with explicit setup/teardown and replayable seeds.
- Compare FrankenLibC outputs against host glibc fixtures (or documented replacement-level contract) for all touched symbols.

3. Logging + Traceability Evidence
- Emit structured logs (JSONL or deterministic text) that include bead ID, scenario ID, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, and outcome.
- Verify logs expose repair/deny counters and reason codes so regressions are diagnosable from artifacts alone.
- Record artifact paths in bead notes (test logs, fixture diffs, benchmark outputs, conformance reports).

4. Performance + Regression Evidence
- Capture before/after baseline for latency/throughput on touched hot paths.
- Enforce no-regression threshold or document bounded tradeoff with quantitative rationale.

5. CI Gate Evidence
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo check --all-targets`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-targets`
- all bead-specific harness and E2E scripts pass with deterministic logs archived.","notes":"## Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated for happy-path, edge-case, and invalid-input behavior.
- [ ] Property-style or fuzz-seed replay coverage added where state space is large/combinatorial.
- [ ] Integration fixtures added/updated in `tests/conformance` and validated against host glibc expectations.
- [ ] Deterministic E2E script/runner added or updated with explicit command lines and seed controls.
- [ ] Strict vs hardened behavior assertions are explicit and versioned in fixtures.
- [ ] Structured logs include: timestamp, bead ID, test scenario, runtime mode, symbol family, decision path, action (allow/repair/deny), and result.
- [ ] Failure logs include reason codes + minimal reproducible input payload metadata.
- [ ] Benchmark/perf logs include baseline, candidate, delta %, and pass/fail gate decision.
- [ ] CI artifacts include conformance report, E2E transcript, and summarized pass/fail matrix.
- [ ] Release/merge gate blocked if any required test or log artifact is missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.584467417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:56:39.065467719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-13T23:56:39.065262901Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","iconv","logging","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z7gt","depends_on_id":"bd-13ya","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7gt","depends_on_id":"bd-by8c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
